---
title: "draft"
format: html
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup}
# Load libraries from description file
pak::pak()

# library(here)
# library(readr)
# library(dplyr)
# library(tibble)
# library(ggrepel)
# library(tidyverse)
# library(limma)
# library(DESeq2)
# library(pheatmap)
# library(ggforce)
# library(ggplot2)
# library(ggrepel)
# library(RColorBrewer)
# library(enrichplot)
# library(clusterProfiler)
# library(org.Mm.eg.db)
# library(stringr)
# library(ComplexHeatmap)
# library(circlize)
# 
# here()

source("~/Documents/RProjects/2026_AAV-TuD_2wDIOCohort/R/functions.R")
```

```{r Import data}
meta <- import_meta_data("meta/metadata.csv")

raw_counts_matrix <- import_raw_counts_matrix("data-raw/salmon.merged.gene_counts.tsv", meta)


# Make a gene list using annotations from raw_counts_matrix
genes_annot <- raw_counts_matrix |>
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene_id") |>
  dplyr::select("gene_id", "gene_name")


# Get mouse annotations
# Load libraries
library(AnnotationHub)
library(ensembldb)

# Connect to AnnotationHub
ah <- AnnotationHub()
ah

# Query AnnotationHub
mouse_ens <- query(ah, c("Mus musculus", "EnsDb", "GRCm39"))
mouse_ens

# Extract annotations of interest (as of Apr 2026: GRCm39, release 113)
# If you want GRCm38, use release 102.
mouse_ens <- mouse_ens[["AH116909"]] # Ensembl release 112 for Mus Musculus; release 113 not available yet.

# Extract gene-level information
genes(mouse_ens, return.type = "data.frame")

# Extract transcript-level information
transcripts(mouse_ens, return.type = "data.frame")

# Extract exon-level information
exons(mouse_ens, return.type = "data.frame")

# Create a gene-level dataframe based on the genes from the raw_counts matrix
annotations_ahb <- genes(mouse_ens, return.type = "data.frame") |>
  dplyr::select(gene_id, gene_name, entrezid, gene_biotype, description) |>
  dplyr::filter(gene_id %in% genes_annot$gene_id)
```

```{r Data filtering}
iWAT_meta <- meta |>
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "iWAT")

iWAT_meta$miRNA_scaled <- scale(iWAT_meta$miRNA_level)

iWAT_samples <- meta |>
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "iWAT") |>
  dplyr::pull(sample_name)

iWAT_raw_counts <- raw_counts_matrix |>
  dplyr::select(gene_name, dplyr::all_of(iWAT_samples)) |>
  dplyr::select(-"gene_name") |>
  base::round()

###################### DEseq normalized counts
all(colnames(iWAT_raw_counts) %in% rownames(iWAT_meta))
all(colnames(iWAT_raw_counts) == rownames(iWAT_meta))


## Create DESeq2Dataset object
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = iWAT_raw_counts,
  colData = iWAT_meta,
  design = ~group
)
```


```{r PCA}
######################## PCA plots
# Transform counts for data visualization
rld <- rlog(dds, blind = TRUE)

# Basic PCA plot
plotPCA(rld, intgroup = "group")

# Extract PCA data
pca_data <- plotPCA(rld, intgroup = "group", returnData = TRUE)

# Add sample names as a column
pca_data$sample_name <- rownames(pca_data)

# Calculate variance explained
percentVar <- round(100 * attr(pca_data, "percentVar"))

# Plot PCA with ellipses
ggplot(pca_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = group, label = sample_name)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) + # Add points
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#54494B", "#E5C689")) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = group), type = "t", level = 0.95) +
  xlab(paste0("PC1: ", percentVar[1], "% variance")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2: ", percentVar[2], "% variance")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  geom_label_repel(max.overlaps = 20)


## Plot PCA for other PC's (PC3 and PC4)
# Input a matrix of log-transformed values
# rld <- rlog(dds, blind=TRUE)
rld_mat <- assay(rld)
pca <- prcomp(t(rld_mat))

percentVar <- (pca$sdev^2) / sum(pca$sdev^2)

# Create data frame with metadata and PC3 and PC4 values for input to ggplot
df <- cbind(iWAT_meta, pca$x)

ggplot(df, aes(x = PC5, y = PC6, color = group, label = rownames(df))) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  stat_ellipse(type = "norm", level = 0.95) +
  geom_label_repel(max.overlaps = 20) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#54494B", "#E5C689")) +
  xlab(paste0("PC5: ", round(100 * percentVar[5], 1), "% variance")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC6: ", round(100 * percentVar[6], 1), "% variance")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

ggplot(df, aes(x = PC3, y = PC4, color = group, label = rownames(df))) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  stat_ellipse(type = "norm", level = 0.95) +
  geom_label_repel(max.overlaps = 20) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#54494B", "#E5C689")) +
  xlab(paste0("PC3: ", round(100 * percentVar[3], 1), "% variance")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC4: ", round(100 * percentVar[4], 1), "% variance")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

ggplot(df, aes(x = PC7, y = PC8, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", level = 0.95) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#54494B", "#E5C689")) +
  xlab(paste0("PC7: ", round(100 * percentVar[7], 1), "% variance")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC8: ", round(100 * percentVar[8], 1), "% variance")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

ggplot(df, aes(x = PC9, y = PC10, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", level = 0.95) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#54494B", "#E5C689")) +
  xlab(paste0("PC9: ", round(100 * percentVar[9], 1), "% variance")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC10: ", round(100 * percentVar[10], 1), "% variance")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

```{r Heatmap to check replicate correlation}
norm_counts <- counts(dds, normalized = TRUE)
vsd <- vst(dds, blind = TRUE)

mat <- assay(vsd)
sample_cor <- cor(mat)

pheatmap(sample_cor,
  clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",
  clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
  main = "Sample-to-sample correlation"
)
```


```{r Data cleanup}
### Choose only samples with confirmed KD in TM assay
iWAT_meta <- meta |>
  dplyr::filter_out(mouse_ID %in% c(
    "JWK-0408",
    "JWK-0404",
    "JWK-0376",
    "JWK-0372"
  ))

iWAT_samples <- iWAT_meta |>
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "iWAT") |>
  dplyr::pull(sample_name)

iWAT_raw_counts <- raw_counts_matrix |>
  dplyr::select(gene_name, dplyr::all_of(iWAT_samples)) |>
  dplyr::select(-"gene_name") |>
  base::round()


iWAT_meta_clean <- iWAT_meta |>
  dplyr::filter(mouse_ID %in% c(
    "JWK-0395",
    "JWK-0397",
    "JWK-0407",
    "JWK-0372",
    "JWK-0400",
    "JWK-0401",
    "JWK-0402",
    "JWK-0378"
  ))

iWAT_samples <- iWAT_meta_clean |>
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "iWAT") |>
  dplyr::pull(sample_name)

iWAT_raw_counts <- raw_counts_matrix |>
  dplyr::select(gene_name, dplyr::all_of(iWAT_samples)) |>
  dplyr::select(-"gene_name") |>
  base::round()


###################### DEseq normalized counts
all(colnames(iWAT_raw_counts) %in% rownames(iWAT_meta_clean))
all(colnames(iWAT_raw_counts) == rownames(iWAT_meta_clean))

## Create DESeq2Dataset object
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = iWAT_raw_counts,
  colData = iWAT_meta_clean,
  design = ~group
)
```


```{r PCA - extended}
########### KEGG pathway analysis on top 200 genes driving PC1 and PC2
## Asks the question: are these genes enriched in pathways?

## Extract top genes driving PC1 and PC2

pc1_loadings <- pca$rotation[, "PC1"]

top_pc1_genes <- sort(abs(pc1_loadings), decreasing = TRUE)[1:20]


pc2_loadings <- pca$rotation[, "PC2"]

top_pc2_genes <- sort(abs(pc2_loadings), decreasing = TRUE)[1:20]

top_pc1 <- names(top_pc1_genes)
top_pc2 <- names(top_pc2_genes)

## Convert genes to a table
pc1_table <- data.frame(
  gene = names(pc1_loadings),
  loading = pc1_loadings
)
pc1_table <- pc1_table[order(abs(pc1_table$loading), decreasing = TRUE), ]
head(pc1_table, 20)

pc2_table <- data.frame(
  gene = names(pc2_loadings),
  loading = pc2_loadings
)
pc2_table <- pc2_table[order(abs(pc2_table$loading), decreasing = TRUE), ]
head(pc2_table, 20)

## Annotate the table with annotations from annotations_ahb
pc1_annot <- pc1_table |>
  left_join(annotations_ahb, by = c("gene" = "gene_id"))
head(pc1_annot, 20)
head(pc1_annot[, c("gene_name", "loading")], 20)

pc2_annot <- pc2_table |>
  left_join(annotations_ahb, by = c("gene" = "gene_id"))
head(pc2_annot, 20)

# Check
sum(is.na(pc1_annot$gene_name))
sum(is.na(pc2_annot$gene_name))

## Arrange genes and cutoff first 200

top_pc1 <- pc1_annot |>
  arrange(desc(abs(loading))) |>
  slice(1:200)

top_pc2 <- pc2_annot |>
  arrange(desc(abs(loading))) |>
  slice(1:200)

## KEGG pathway analysis

pc1_kegg_genes <- pc1_annot %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(entrezid))

pc2_kegg_genes <- pc2_annot %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(entrezid))

kegg_res_pc1 <- enrichKEGG(
  gene         = top_pc1$entrezid,
  organism     = "mmu",
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05
)

kegg_res_pc2 <- enrichKEGG(
  gene         = top_pc2$entrezid,
  organism     = "mmu",
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05
)

dotplot(kegg_res_pc1,
  showCategory = 15,
  title = "KEGG Pathways in PC1"
)
dotplot(kegg_res_pc2,
  showCategory = 15,
  title = "KEGG Pathways in PC2"
)


############ GSEA on ranked genes
## Ranks all genes in the loadings and asks: are genes from a pathway concentrated at the top or bottom of a ranked list?
## Ranks genes from high to low, then asks: do KEGG pathway genes cluster toward top or bottom?
gene_ranks_pc1 <- pc1_annot$loading
names(gene_ranks_pc1) <- pc1_annot$entrezid

gene_ranks_pc1 <- gene_ranks_pc1[!is.na(names(gene_ranks_pc1))]
gene_ranks_pc1 <- sort(gene_ranks_pc1, decreasing = TRUE)

gse_kegg_pc1 <- gseKEGG(
  geneList = gene_ranks_pc1,
  organism = "mmu"
)

gseaplot2(gse_kegg_pc1, geneSetID = 1:5)
dotplot(gse_kegg_pc1, showCategory = 15)


gene_ranks_pc2 <- pc2_annot$loading
names(gene_ranks_pc2) <- pc2_annot$entrezid

gene_ranks_pc2 <- gene_ranks_pc2[!is.na(names(gene_ranks_pc2))]
gene_ranks_pc2 <- sort(gene_ranks_pc2, decreasing = TRUE)

gse_kegg_pc2 <- gseKEGG(
  geneList = gene_ranks_pc1,
  organism = "mmu"
)

gseaplot2(gse_kegg_pc2, geneSetID = 1:5)
dotplot(gse_kegg_pc2, showCategory = 15)


# Extract data and look at NES scores
# If NES > 0, pathway is enriched at positive PC1 loadings. Means that genes in this pathway are higher on the right side of the PCA axis.
# If NES <0, pathway is enriched at negative PC1 loadings. Means that genes in ths pathway are higher on the left side of the PCA axis.
gsea_df_pc1 <- as.data.frame(gse_kegg_pc1)
gsea_sig_pc1 <- gsea_df_pc1 |>
  dplyr::filter(p.adjust < 0.05)
top_pos_pc1 <- gsea_sig_pc1 |>
  dplyr::arrange(desc(NES)) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1:5)
top_neg_pc1 <- gsea_sig_pc1 |>
  dplyr::arrange(NES) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1:5)
gsea_top_pc1 <- dplyr::bind_rows(top_pos_pc1, top_neg_pc1)

gsea_df_pc2 <- as.data.frame(gse_kegg_pc2)
gsea_sig_pca2 <- gsea_df_pc2 |>
  dplyr::filter(p.adjust < 0.05)

top_pos_pc2 <- gsea_sig_pca2 |>
  dplyr::arrange(desc(NES)) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1:5)

top_neg_pc2 <- gsea_sig_pca2 |>
  dplyr::arrange(NES) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1:5)

gsea_top_pc2 <- dplyr::bind_rows(top_pos_pc2, top_neg_pc2)


### Plot
gsea_top_pc1 <- gsea_top_pc1 %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Description_clean = gsub(" - Mus musculus \\(house mouse\\)", "", Description),
    Description_clean = str_wrap(Description_clean, width = 20)
  )

ggplot(gsea_top_pc1, aes(x = NES, y = reorder(Description_clean, NES))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = setSize, color = p.adjust)) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#B4C7D6", high = "#A94642") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-2, 2)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.1))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "black")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "PC1 GSEA Pathway Enrichment",
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    y = NULL,
    color = "adjusted p-value",
    size = "Gene Set Size"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 5),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.2, "cm"),
    legend.position = "right"
  ) # +
# guides(
#   size = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2)),
#   color = guide_colorbar(barwidth = 4, barheight = 0.3)
# ) # for if you want the legends to be horizontal


gsea_top_pc2 <- gsea_top_pc2 %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Description_clean = gsub(" - Mus musculus \\(house mouse\\)", "", Description),
    Description_clean = str_wrap(Description_clean, width = 20)
  )

ggplot(gsea_top_pc2, aes(x = NES, y = reorder(Description_clean, NES))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = setSize, color = p.adjust)) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#B4C7D6", high = "#A94642") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-2, 2)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.1))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "black")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "PC2 GSEA Pathway Enrichment",
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    y = NULL,
    color = "adjusted p-value",
    size = "Gene Set Size"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 5),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.2, "cm"),
    legend.position = "right"
  ) # +
# guides(
#   size = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2)),
#   color = guide_colorbar(barwidth = 4, barheight = 0.3)
# ) # for if you want the legends to be horizontal
```

```{r DE analysis}
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = iWAT_raw_counts,
  colData = iWAT_meta,
  design = ~group
)

###################### DESeq
## Outputs a results table with all DE genes

# Perform the DE analysis
dds <- DESeq(dds)

resultsNames(dds)
# Make sure that the control is at the beginning of the levels
dds$group
# dds$condition <- relevel(dds$condition, ref = "untreated")

# Extract results table
contrast <- c("group", "miR-335-TuD_6w", "ctrl-TuD_6w")
res_table <- results(dds, contrast = contrast, alpha = 0.05) # FDR > 0.05

# Filter genes by zero expression
res_table |>
  data.frame() |>
  dplyr::filter(baseMean == 0)

# Filter genes that have an extreme outlier
res_table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  dplyr::filter(
    is.na(pvalue),
    is.na(padj),
    baseMean > 0
  )

# Filter genes below the low mean threshold
res_table |>
  data.frame() |>
  dplyr::filter(
    !is.na(pvalue),
    # is.na(padj),
    baseMean > 0
  )

# LFC shrinking
# Save the unshrunken results to compare
res_table_unshrunken <- res_table

# Apply fold change shrinkage
res_table <- lfcShrink(dds, coef = "group_miR.335.TuD_6w_vs_ctrl.TuD_6w", type = "apeglm")
# In the unshrunken fold change values plotted, you can see the abundance of scatter for the the lowly expressed genes.
# That is, many of the low expressors exhibit very high fold changes.
# After shrinkage, we see the fold changes are much smaller estimates.

# Check how many DEGs were retreived
summary(res_table, alpha = 0.05) # padj/FDR < 0.05

# Compare with unshrunken
summary(res_table_unshrunken, alpha = 0.05)

# MA plot
plotMA(res_table, ylim = c(-2, 2))
plotMA(res_table_unshrunken, ylim = c(-2, 2))

padj.cutoff <- 0.05

# Create a tibble of results and add back gene ids
res_table_tb <- res_table |>
  data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene_id") |>
  as_tibble()

# Add gene_name from  original counts matrix
res_table_tb <- res_table_tb |>
  left_join(
    raw_counts_matrix |>
      rownames_to_column(var = "gene_id") |>
      dplyr::select(gene_id, gene_name),
    by = "gene_id"
  )

# Subset the tibble to keep only significant genes (padj<0.05)
sig_kd <- res_table_tb |>
  dplyr::filter(padj < padj.cutoff)

# Take a quick look at this tibble
sig_kd

# Make a csv file
# write_csv(sig_kd, "results/sig_kd.csv")
```

```{r DE visualization}
# Add the gene symbols column to the normalized_counts tibble
normalized_counts <- counts(dds, normalized = T) |> # First convert normalized_counts to a data frame
  data.frame(check.names = F) |> # Prevents column names from changing
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene_id") |> # Transfer the row names to a new column called "gene_id"
  as_tibble() |>
  left_join(
    raw_counts_matrix |>
      rownames_to_column(var = "gene_id") |>
      dplyr::select(gene_id, gene_name),
    by = "gene_id"
  )


# Save as file
# write_csv(normalized_counts, "results/normalized_counts.csv")

## Volcano plot
# Obtain logical vector where TRUE values denote padj values < 0.05 and fold change > 1.5 in either direction
# 2^0.585 ≈ 1.5
res_table_tb <- res_table_tb |>
  dplyr::mutate(threshold_de = padj < 0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) >= 0.585)

## Create an empty column to indicate which genes to label
res_table_tb <- res_table_tb |> dplyr::mutate(genelabels = "")

## Sort by padj values
res_table_tb <- res_table_tb |> dplyr::arrange(padj)

## Populate the genelabels column with contents of the gene symbols column for the first 10 rows, i.e. the top 30 most significantly expressed genes
res_table_tb$genelabels[1:30] <- as.character(res_table_tb$gene_name[1:30])

## OR Populate genelabels with all genes that pass threshold
res_table_tb <- res_table_tb |>
  mutate(threshold_de = threshold_de)

res_table_tb <- res_table_tb |>
  mutate(genelabels = ifelse(threshold_de, as.character(gene_name), NA))

ggplot(res_table_tb, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj))) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = threshold_de),
    alpha = 0.7,
    size = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_label_repel(aes(label = genelabels), size = 2, max.overlaps = 20) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#B4C7D6", "#A94642")) +
  # scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-10, 10), expand = expansion(mult = 0.02)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.585, 0.585), linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  ggtitle("miR-335 KD in 2w DIO iWAT") +
  xlab("log2 fold change") +
  ylab("-log10 adjusted p-value") +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(-3, 10),
    ylim = c(0, 10)
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(size = rel(1.5), hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    axis.text = element_text(size = rel(0.8)),
    base_size = 8
  )


############### Heatmap ###############
# Filter significant genes
sig_gene_names <- sig_kd |>
  dplyr::select("gene_id") |>
  pull()

# Extract the expression matrix of rlog-transformed values stored in the DEseq2 transformed object and add gene annotations.
# These values are in log2-scale, already normalize, and are variance-stabilized.
# heatmap_data <- assay(rld)[sig_gene_names, ]
heatmap_data <- assay(rld) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  tibble::rownames_to_column("gene") |>
  dplyr::filter(gene %in% sig_gene_names) |>
  tibble::column_to_rownames("gene")

breaks <- seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101)

# Build heatmap matrix (keep gene_id as rownames)
heatmap_data <- assay(rld) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  tibble::rownames_to_column("gene_id") |>
  dplyr::filter(gene_id %in% sig_gene_names) |>
  tibble::column_to_rownames("gene_id")

# Create row labels from annotation table
labels_row <- annotations_ahb$gene_name[
  match(rownames(heatmap_data), annotations_ahb$gene_id)
]

# Fallback to gene_id if gene_name is missing
labels_row[is.na(labels_row)] <- rownames(heatmap_data)[is.na(labels_row)]


pheatmap(
  heatmap_data,
  labels_row = labels_row,
  annotation_col = iWAT_meta[, "group", drop = FALSE],
  annotation_colors = list(
    group = c(
      "ctrl-TuD_6w" = "#54494B",
      "miR-335-TuD_6w" = "#E5C689"
    )
  ),
  color = colorRampPalette(c("#B4C7D6", "white", "#A94642"))(100),
  # breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
  scale = "row",
  clustering_distance_rows = "correlation",
  clustering_distance_cols = "correlation",
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  border_color = NA,
  show_colnames = FALSE,
  main = "DESeq2 Significant Genes (row-scaled)",
  name = "Scaled expression (z-score)",
  legend_breaks = seq(-2, 2, by = 1),
  legend_labels = c("-2", "-1", "0", "1", "2"),
  fontsize = 8
)

##### With ComplexHeatmap
# -----------------------------
# 1. Filter significant genes
# -----------------------------
sig_gene_names <- sig_kd |>
  dplyr::select(gene_id) |>
  dplyr::pull()

# -----------------------------
# 2. Build heatmap matrix
# -----------------------------
heatmap_data <- assay(rld) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  tibble::rownames_to_column("gene_id") |>
  dplyr::filter(gene_id %in% sig_gene_names) |>
  tibble::column_to_rownames("gene_id")

# -----------------------------
# 3. Row scaling (z-score)
# -----------------------------
heatmap_scaled <- t(scale(t(as.matrix(heatmap_data))))

# -----------------------------
# 4. Gene name labels (optional)
# -----------------------------
labels_row <- annotations_ahb$gene_name[
  match(rownames(heatmap_scaled), annotations_ahb$gene_id)
]

labels_row[is.na(labels_row)] <- rownames(heatmap_scaled)

# -----------------------------
# 5. Column annotation
# -----------------------------
group_colors <- c(
  "ctrl-TuD_6w" = "#54494B",
  "miR-335-TuD_6w" = "#E5C689"
)

top_anno <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  group = iWAT_meta$group,
  col = list(group = group_colors),
  show_annotation_name = FALSE
)

# -----------------------------
# 6. Color function (z-score)
# -----------------------------
col_fun <- circlize::colorRamp2(
  c(-2, 0, 2),
  c("#B4C7D6", "white", "#A94642")
)

# -----------------------------
# 7. Build heatmap
# -----------------------------
ht <- Heatmap(
  heatmap_scaled,
  name = "z-score",
  col = col_fun,
  top_annotation = top_anno,
  show_row_names = TRUE,
  row_labels = labels_row,
  show_column_names = FALSE,
  clustering_distance_rows = "pearson",
  clustering_distance_columns = "euclidean",

  # Create gene modules
  # row_split = 9, # try 3–6 depending on complexity

  column_title = "DESeq2 Significant Genes (row-scaled)",
  border = FALSE,
  column_split = iWAT_meta$group
)

# -----------------------------
# 8. Extract which genes are in which modules
# -----------------------------
ht_drawn <- draw(ht)

row_clusters <- row_order(ht_drawn)

module_list <- lapply(seq_along(row_clusters), function(i) {
  data.frame(
    gene_id = rownames(heatmap_scaled)[row_clusters[[i]]],
    module = paste0("Module_", i)
  )
}) |> dplyr::bind_rows()

# -----------------------------
# 9. Annotate the modules
# -----------------------------

module7_genes <- module_list |>
  dplyr::filter(module == "Module_7") |>
  dplyr::pull(gene_id)

ego7 <- enrichGO(
  gene = module7_genes,
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
  keyType = "ENSEMBL",
  ont = "BP"
)

module7_summary <- data.frame(ego7)

module7_genes_tb <- tibble::tibble(gene_id = module7_genes) |>
  dplyr::left_join(annotations_ahb, by = "gene_id")

# Expand geneID column (ENSEMBL IDs separated by "/")

ego7_long <- module7_summary %>%
  dplyr::select(ID, Description, geneID) %>%
  tidyr::separate_rows(geneID, sep = "/") %>%
  dplyr::rename(gene_id = geneID)

ego7_annotated <- ego7_long %>%
  dplyr::left_join(annotations_ahb, by = "gene_id")

module4_genes <- module_list |>
  dplyr::filter(module == "Module_4") |>
  dplyr::pull(gene_id)

ego <- enrichGO(
  gene = module4_genes,
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
  keyType = "ENSEMBL",
  ont = "BP"
)

module4_summary <- data.frame(ego)
module4_genes_tb <- tibble::tibble(gene_id = module4_genes) |>
  dplyr::left_join(annotations_ahb, by = "gene_id")


module6_genes <- module_list |>
  dplyr::filter(module == "Module_6") |>
  dplyr::pull(gene_id)

ego6 <- enrichGO(
  gene = module6_genes,
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
  keyType = "ENSEMBL",
  ont = "BP"
)

module6_summary <- data.frame(ego6)

module6_genes_tb <- tibble::tibble(gene_id = module6_genes) |>
  dplyr::left_join(annotations_ahb, by = "gene_id")

module1_genes <- module_list |>
  dplyr::filter(module == "Module_1") |>
  dplyr::pull(gene_id)

ego1 <- enrichGO(
  gene = module1_genes,
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
  keyType = "ENSEMBL",
  ont = "BP"
)

module1_summary <- data.frame(ego1)
module1_genes_tb <- tibble::tibble(gene_id = module1_genes) |>
  dplyr::left_join(annotations_ahb, by = "gene_id")


### Add module labels to heatmap

module_names <- c(
  "Stromal/vascular identity",
  "Stress & remodeling",
  "Inflammation"
)

ht <- Heatmap(
  heatmap_scaled,
  name = "z-score",
  col = col_fun,
  top_annotation = top_anno,
  show_row_names = TRUE,
  row_labels = labels_row,
  show_column_names = FALSE,
  clustering_distance_rows = "pearson",
  clustering_distance_columns = "pearson",
  row_split = 3,
  row_title = module_names, # Add module labels
  row_title_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 5, fontface = "bold"),
  row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 5),
  column_title = "DESeq2 Significant Genes (row-scaled)",
  column_title_gp = grid::gpar(fontface = "bold"),
  row_title_side = "right",
  border = FALSE,
  column_split = iWAT_meta$group,
)

ht_drawn <- draw(ht)


############### Clustering ###############
## Identifying clusters of genes with shared expression profiles
# Extract results for LRT
dds_lrt <- DESeq(dds, test = "LRT", reduced = ~1)

res_LRT <- results(dds_lrt)
resultsNames(dds_lrt)
dds_lrt$group
res_LRT

# Identifying significant genes
# Create a tibble for LRT results
res_LRT_tb <- res_LRT %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene_id") %>%
  as_tibble()

# Subset to return genes with padj < 0.05
sigLRT_genes <- res_LRT_tb %>%
  dplyr::filter(padj < padj.cutoff)

# Get number of significant genes
nrow(sigLRT_genes)

# Compare to numbers we had from Wald test
nrow(sig_kd)

# Inner join to find overlapping genes
overlapping_genes <- inner_join(sig_kd, sigLRT_genes, by = "gene_id")

# Count the number of overlapping genes
nrow(overlapping_genes)



### Transform counts for data visualization
rld <- rlog(dds, blind = TRUE)
rld_mat <- assay(rld)

# Subset results for faster cluster finding (for classroom demo purposes)
clustering_sig_genes <- sigLRT_genes |>
  arrange(padj) |>
  head(n = 1000)


# Obtain rlog values for those significant genes
cluster_rlog <- rld_mat[clustering_sig_genes$gene_id, ]

# Use the `degPatterns` function from the 'DEGreport' package to show gene clusters across conditions
library(DEGreport)
clusters <- degPatterns(cluster_rlog,
  metadata = iWAT_meta_clean,
  time = "group",
  col = NULL
)


# Extract the Group 1 genes --> genes that increase in expression from miR-335 KD, compared to control
group1 <- clusters$df |>
  dplyr::filter(cluster == 1) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    gene_name = genes_annot$gene_name[
      match(genes, genes_annot$gene_id)
    ]
  )

# Extract the Group 2 genes --> genes that decrease in expression from miR-335 KD, compared to control
group2 <- clusters$df |>
  dplyr::filter(cluster == 2) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    gene_name = genes_annot$gene_name[
      match(genes, genes_annot$gene_id)
    ]
  )


# Create a matrix for Group 1 genes
group1_mat <- cluster_rlog[group1$genes, ]
rownames(group1_mat) <- group1$gene_name # Replace gene IDs with gene names

# Optional: scale by row (genes) for better visualization
group1_scaled <- t(scale(t(group1_mat)))

# Plot heatmap for Group 1
pheatmap(
  group1_scaled,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  color = colorRampPalette(c("navy", "white", "firebrick3"))(50),
  main = "Cluster 1: Downregulated Genes"
)

# Repeat for Group 2 genes
group2_mat <- cluster_rlog[group2$genes, ]
rownames(group2_mat) <- group2$gene_name
group2_scaled <- t(scale(t(group2_mat)))


pheatmap(
  group2_scaled,
  annotation_col = iWAT_meta_clean[, "group", drop = FALSE],
  annotation_colors = list(
    group = c(
      "ctrl-TuD_6w" = "#54494B",
      "miR-335-TuD_6w" = "#E5C689"
    )
  ),
  annotation_legend = FALSE,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = FALSE,
  # color = colorRampPalette(c("navy", "white", "firebrick3"))(50),
  color = colorRampPalette(c("#B4C7D6", "white", "#A94642"))(100),
  breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
  border_color = NA,
  main = "Cluster 2: Upregulated Genes"
)

####### Using top variable genes per cluster
### 1. rlog transform
rld <- rlog(dds, blind = TRUE)
rld_mat <- assay(rld)

### 2. keep all genes for clustering (NO DE filtering)
gene_var <- apply(rld_mat, 1, var)

top_genes <- names(sort(gene_var, decreasing = TRUE))[1:2000]
top_genes <- names(sort(apply(rld_mat, 1, var), decreasing = TRUE))[1:2000]

cluster_mat <- rld_mat[top_genes, ]

### 3. clustering
clusters <- degPatterns(
  cluster_mat,
  metadata = iWAT_meta_clean,
  time = "group",
  col = NULL
)

### 4. select cluster 1 genes
cluster1 <- clusters$df |>
  dplyr::filter(cluster == 1)

### 5. extract expression matrix for cluster 2
group1_mat <- rld_mat[cluster1$genes, ]

### 6. select TOP VARIABLE GENES within cluster (IMPORTANT STEP)
gene_var1 <- apply(group1_mat, 1, var)

top_cluster1_genes <- names(sort(gene_var1, decreasing = TRUE))[1:60]

group1_small <- group1_mat[top_cluster1_genes, ]

### 7. scale rows (z-score per gene)
group1_scaled <- t(scale(t(group1_small)))


### 8. Annotate with gene names
labels_row <- annotations_ahb$gene_name[
  match(rownames(group1_scaled), annotations_ahb$gene_id)
]

labels_row[is.na(labels_row)] <- rownames(group1_scaled)

### 8. heatmap
pheatmap(
  group1_scaled,
  annotation_col = iWAT_meta_clean[, "group", drop = FALSE],
  annotation_colors = list(
    group = c(
      "ctrl-TuD_6w" = "#54494B",
      "miR-335-TuD_6w" = "#E5C689"
    )
  ),
  row_labels = labels_row,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = FALSE,
  color = colorRampPalette(c("#B4C7D6", "white", "#A94642"))(100),
  breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
  border_color = NA,
  main = "Cluster 1 (Top 60 variable genes)"
)
```

```{r ORA - GO analysis}
## Untested genes have padj = NA, so let's keep genes with padj != NA
res_table_tb_noNAs <- dplyr::filter(res_table_tb, padj != "NA")

res_ids <- res_table_tb_noNAs |>
  dplyr::select(-"gene_name")

## Create background dataset for hypergeometric testing using all tested genes for significance in the results
all_genes <- as.character(genes_annot$gene_id)

## Extract significant results
sig_kd <- dplyr::filter(res_ids, padj < 0.05)

sig_kd_genes <- as.character(sig_kd$gene_id)


## Run GO enrichment analysis
ego_bp <- enrichGO(
  gene = sig_kd_genes,
  universe = all_genes,
  keyType = "ENSEMBL",
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
  ont = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  readable = TRUE
)

## Output results from GO analysis to a table
cluster_summary_bp <- data.frame(ego_bp)

# write.csv(cluster_summary, "results/clusterProfiler.csv")


dotplot(ego_bp, showCategory = 30)

## Run GO enrichment analysis
ego_mf <- enrichGO(
  gene = sig_kd_genes,
  universe = all_genes,
  keyType = "ENSEMBL",
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
  ont = "MF",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  readable = TRUE
)

## Output results from GO analysis to a table
cluster_summary_mf <- data.frame(ego_mf)

dotplot(ego_mf, showCategory = 20)

# Map to Entrez IDs
sig_kd_genes_entrez <- bitr(sig_kd$gene_id,
  fromType = "ENSEMBL",
  toType = "ENTREZID",
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db
)

kegg_enrich <- enrichKEGG(
  gene = sig_kd_genes_entrez$ENTREZID,
  organism = "mmu", # mouse
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05
)

# View top pathways
head(kegg_enrich)

# Plot barplot or dotplot
library(enrichplot)
barplot(kegg_enrich, showCategory = 20)
dotplot(kegg_enrich, showCategory = 20)



##### Run GO analyses on up/downregulated genes separately
## Extract upregulated genes
sig_up <- dplyr::filter(res_ids, padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange > 0)

sig_up_genes <- as.character(sig_up$gene_id)

## Run GO enrichment analysis
ego_up <- enrichGO(
  gene = sig_up_genes,
  universe = all_genes,
  keyType = "ENSEMBL",
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
  ont = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  readable = TRUE
)

simplify(ego_up)

ego_up_bp <- data.frame(ego_up)

dotplot(ego_up, showCategory = 20)

## Extract downregulated genes
sig_down <- dplyr::filter(res_ids, padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange < 0)

sig_down_genes <- as.character(sig_down$gene_id)
```

```{r fGSEA}
# res is your DESeq2 results
res_df <- res_table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("gene_id") |>
  left_join(
    raw_counts_matrix |>
      rownames_to_column("gene_id") |>
      dplyr::select(gene_id, gene_name),
    by = "gene_id"
  )

# Remove NAs
res_df <- res_df |>
  dplyr::filter(
    !is.na(pvalue),
    !is.na(log2FoldChange),
    !is.na(gene_name),
    gene_name != ""
  )

# Create custom ranking for GSEA; a significance-weighted expression change score
library(msigdbr)
library(curl)
library(fgsea)
msig <- msigdbr(species = "Mus musculus", collection = "H")

ranks <- sign(res_df$log2FoldChange) * -log10(res_df$pvalue)
names(ranks) <- res_df$gene_name
sum(names(ranks) %in% unique(msig$gene_symbol))



pathways <- split(msig$gene_symbol, msig$gs_name)

fgseaRes <- fgsea(
  pathways = pathways,
  stats = ranks,
  minSize = 15,
  maxSize = 500
)

fgseaRes <- fgseaRes[order(fgseaRes$padj), ]

head(fgseaRes[, c("pathway", "NES", "padj")])
tail(fgseaRes[, c("pathway", "NES", "padj")])


### Or use raw unshrunken LFC directly
ranks <- res_df$log2FoldChange
names(ranks) <- res_df$gene_name

ranks <- ranks[!is.na(ranks)]
ranks <- sort(ranks, decreasing = TRUE)

fgseaRes <- fgsea(
  pathways = pathways,
  stats = ranks,
  minSize = 15,
  maxSize = 500
)


##### Get leading edge genes for a specific pathway
fgseaRes$leadingEdge[fgseaRes$pathway == "HALLMARK_FATTY_ACID_METABOLISM"][[1]]
fgseaRes$leadingEdge[fgseaRes$pathway == "HALLMARK_PI3K_AKT_MTOR_SIGNALING"][[1]]
fgseaRes$leadingEdge[fgseaRes$pathway == "HALLMARK_ADIPOGENESIS"][[1]]
fgseaRes$leadingEdge[fgseaRes$pathway == "HALLMARK_OXIDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION"][[1]]
fgseaRes$leadingEdge[fgseaRes$pathway == "HALLMARK_GLYCOLYSIS"][[1]]

##### Plot
plotEnrichment(pathways[["HALLMARK_OXIDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION"]], ranks)

topPathways <- fgseaRes[order(padj)][1:5, pathway]

lapply(topPathways, function(pw) {
  plotEnrichment(pathways[[pw]], ranks) + ggtitle(pw)
})

## Custom dotplot
fgseaRes_clean <- fgseaRes %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    pathway = str_remove(pathway, "^HALLMARK_"),
    pathway = str_replace_all(pathway, "_", " ")
  )

fgseaRes_clean$pathway <- stringr::str_to_title(fgseaRes_clean$pathway)

fgseaRes_clean |>
  arrange(padj) |>
  slice(1:30) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = NES, y = reorder(pathway, NES))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = size, color = padj)) +
  ggtitle("FGSEA") +
  labs(y = NULL) +
  labs(x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)") +
  scale_color_gradient(
    low = "#A94642", # most significant
    high = "grey40" # least significant
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) stringr::str_wrap(x, width = 25)) + # wrap texts long pathway names
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9)
  )

## Volcano-like plot
ggplot(fgseaRes, aes(NES, -log10(padj))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal()



##### Get DE genes contributing to a specific pathway
path_genes <- pathways[["HALLMARK_ADIPOGENESIS"]]

intersect(names(ranks), path_genes)

up_genes <- intersect(names(ranks[ranks > 0]), path_genes)
down_genes <- intersect(names(ranks[ranks < 0]), path_genes)

up_genes
down_genes
```

```{r GSEA}
# Map the ensembl ids to entrez ids from the results table

res_ids_entrez <- bitr(res_ids$gene_id,
  fromType = "ENSEMBL",
  toType = "ENTREZID",
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db
)

res_ids <- res_ids |>
  left_join(res_ids_entrez, by = c("gene_id" = "ENSEMBL"))


## Remove any NA values (reduces the data by quite a bit)
res_entrez <- dplyr::filter(res_ids, ENTREZID != "NA")

## Remove any Entrez duplicates
res_entrez <- res_entrez[which(duplicated(res_entrez$ENTREZID) == F), ]

## Extract the foldchanges
foldchanges <- res_entrez$log2FoldChange

## Name each fold change with the corresponding Entrez ID
names(foldchanges) <- res_entrez$ENTREZID

## Sort fold changes in decreasing order
foldchanges <- sort(foldchanges, decreasing = TRUE)

head(foldchanges)

set.seed(123456)

## GSEA using gene sets from KEGG pathways
gseaKEGG <- gseKEGG(
  geneList = foldchanges, # ordered named vector of fold changes (Entrez IDs are the associated names)
  organism = "mmu", # supported organisms listed below
  minGSSize = 20, # minimum gene set size (# genes in set) - change to test more sets or recover sets with fewer # genes
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05, # padj cutoff value
  verbose = FALSE
)

## Extract the GSEA results
gseaKEGG_results <- gseaKEGG@result
head(gseaKEGG_results)
```
